summary: node-procmetrics postinstall installs persistent C2 backdoor
details: The package's postinstall script (install.js, invoked via package.json "postinstall": "node install.js") copies itself to /tmp/.pm-agent.js, spawns a detached background node process, and installs a per-minute crontab entry to relaunch it if not running. The background agent polls http://152.53.120.90/cmd/commands over plain HTTP, executes returned commands via `bash -c` (spawnSync), and POSTs stdout/stderr back to http://152.53.120.90/cmd/results. Multiple network fallbacks (direct, no-proxy, socks5, http-proxy via curl) are attempted to reach the hardcoded IP. The package's advertised purpose as a "system metrics collector" is a cover for a full remote-command-execution backdoor with persistence.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
